options.en: Apply the gait belt snugly over clothing while the client is sitting upright.
options.zh: 患者坐直后，在衣服外贴合地系好步态带。

options.en: Clear the path, lock the bed and wheelchair, place nonskid footwear, and position the walker.
options.zh: 清理通道，锁定床和轮椅，穿防滑鞋，并放好助行器。

options.en: Review the activity order, pain level, recent analgesic timing, and dizziness history.
options.zh: 查看活动医嘱、疼痛程度、近期镇痛药时间和头晕史。

options.en: Use the gait belt and walker to assist the client to stand and pivot to the chair.
options.zh: 使用步态带和助行器，协助患者站立并转身坐到椅子上。

options.en: Assist the client to sit at the bedside and assess dizziness before standing.
options.zh: 协助患者坐在床边，并在站立前评估是否头晕。

questions.topic: Rehabilitation mobility progression and fall prevention

stem.en: For each proposed care-plan item, select whether it supports safe mobility progression.
stem.zh: 针对每项拟定护理计划，选择其是否支持安全活动进展。

correct.en: Safe progression uses scheduled assistance, pain control, proper assistive devices, and call-light reinforcement. Restraint-like strategies and avoidance of all mobility increase harm.
correct.zh: 安全进展包括安排协助、疼痛控制、正确使用辅助器具和强化呼叫铃。类似约束的做法以及完全避免活动会增加伤害。

byChoice.en: Scheduled assisted walks align with the PT plan and build endurance safely.
byChoice.zh: 按计划协助步行符合物理治疗计划，并能安全增强耐力。

byChoice.en: Keeping the client in bed to prevent falls worsens deconditioning and is not a safe rehab plan.
byChoice.zh: 为防跌倒而让患者卧床会加重失能，不是安全康复计划。

byChoice.en: Pre-medicating for pain before therapy can improve participation when sedation risk is monitored.
byChoice.zh: 治疗前适当控制疼痛可改善参与度，但需监测镇静风险。

byChoice.en: A bedside commode with assistance addresses urgency while reducing walking distance during high-risk times.
byChoice.zh: 在协助下使用床旁便器可处理尿急，并在高风险时减少行走距离。

byChoice.en: Raising all side rails to keep the client in bed can function as a restraint and may increase injury risk.
byChoice.zh: 升起所有床栏以限制患者下床可能构成约束，并增加伤害风险。

testTakingStrategy.en: Fall prevention should preserve mobility while reducing risk; immobilizing the client is not rehabilitation.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 跌倒预防应在降低风险的同时保留活动能力；让患者不动不是康复。

glossary.defZh: 卧床或活动减少导致肌力、耐力和平衡下降
帮助患者安全移动的设备，如助行器或手杖

rows.en: Ambulate with walker and one-person assist before meals and at bedtime as tolerated.
rows.zh: 耐受情况下，餐前和睡前使用助行器并由一人协助步行。

rows.en: Keep the client in bed except for therapy sessions to avoid falls.
rows.zh: 除治疗时段外让患者留在床上，以避免跌倒。

rows.en: Coordinate pain medication so the client can participate in therapy while monitoring sedation.
rows.zh: 协调疼痛用药，使患者能参与治疗，同时监测镇静。

rows.en: Use a bedside commode with staff assist overnight because urgency occurs after diuretic use.
rows.zh: 因使用利尿剂后出现尿急，夜间在工作人员协助下使用床旁便器。

rows.en: Raise all four side rails whenever the client is alone.
rows.zh: 患者独处时始终升起四侧床栏。

questions.topic: Rehabilitation mobility progression and fall prevention

stem.en: Use the rehabilitation record to complete the statement.
stem.zh: 根据康复记录完成句子。

correct.en: The client is most at risk for a fall during unassisted toileting because the record shows urgency after diuretic use, attempts to stand without calling, dizziness, and weak gait.
correct.zh: 患者最容易在无人协助如厕时跌倒，因为记录显示利尿剂后尿急、试图不呼叫就站起、头晕和步态无力。

byChoice.en: Fall is correct because multiple exhibit cues point to unsafe unassisted mobility.
byChoice.zh: 跌倒正确，因为多项记录线索指向无人协助活动不安全。

byChoice.en: Unassisted toileting after urgency is the best cause because the client tries to stand quickly without calling.
byChoice.zh: 尿急后无人协助如厕是最佳原因，因为患者会迅速尝试站起而不呼叫。

testTakingStrategy.en: For risk statements, match the outcome to the strongest pattern of cues, not to a diagnosis label alone.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 完成风险句时，将结局与最强线索模式匹配，而不是只看诊断标签。

glossary.defZh: 因虚弱、平衡差、认知或环境因素导致跌倒的可能性

clozeStem.en: The client is at highest risk for {{1}} related to {{2}}.
clozeStem.zh: 患者最高风险是 {{1}}，与 {{2}} 有关。

options.en: fall injury
options.zh: 跌倒伤害

options.en: social isolation
options.zh: 社会隔离

options.en: infection transmission
options.zh: 感染传播

options.en: unassisted toileting after urgency
options.zh: 尿急后无人协助如厕

options.en: refusal to eat breakfast
options.zh: 拒绝吃早餐

options.en: lack of family visits during therapy
options.zh: 治疗期间家属未探视

questions.topic: Rehabilitation mobility progression and fall prevention

stem.en: Which statements by the client indicate understanding of fall-prevention teaching? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 患者哪些话表明理解跌倒预防教学？选择所有适用项。

correct.en: Effective teaching includes calling before standing, using nonskid footwear and the walker, pausing for dizziness, and keeping needed items within reach. Rushing to the bathroom or using furniture instead of the walker is unsafe.
correct.zh: 有效教学包括站起前呼叫、使用防滑鞋和助行器、头晕时暂停，以及把需要物品放在伸手可及处。急着去厕所或扶家具代替助行器是不安全的。

byChoice.en: Furniture walking is unsafe because it provides unstable, inconsistent support.
byChoice.zh: 扶家具走路不安全，因为支撑不稳定且不一致。

byChoice.en: Pausing when dizzy helps detect orthostatic symptoms before walking.
byChoice.zh: 头晕时暂停有助于在行走前发现体位性症状。

byChoice.en: Calling before standing addresses the client's documented attempts to get up alone.
byChoice.zh: 站起前呼叫针对了记录中患者试图独自起身的问题。

byChoice.en: Using the walker and nonskid footwear supports safe mobility.
byChoice.zh: 使用助行器和防滑鞋支持安全活动。

byChoice.en: Rushing because of embarrassment increases fall risk and ignores the toileting plan.
byChoice.zh: 因尴尬而赶路会增加跌倒风险，也忽视了如厕计划。

byChoice.en: Keeping items within reach reduces impulsive unsafe reaching or standing.
byChoice.zh: 把物品放在可及处可减少冲动性不安全伸手或站起。

testTakingStrategy.en: Look for teaching statements that change the exact unsafe behavior shown in the exhibit.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 寻找能改变记录中具体不安全行为的教学表述。

glossary.defZh: 减少足底打滑、帮助预防跌倒的鞋或袜

options.en: “At home, I can hold on to furniture instead of using the walker.”
options.zh: “回家后我可以扶家具，不一定要用助行器。”

options.en: “I will sit at the bedside and tell staff if I feel dizzy before walking.”
options.zh: “走路前我会坐在床边，如果头晕会告诉工作人员。”

options.en: “I will press the call light before I stand, even if I only need the bathroom.”
options.zh: “即使只是要上厕所，我站起来前也会按呼叫铃。”

options.en: “I should wear the nonskid socks and use the walker every time I get up.”
options.zh: “每次起身都应穿防滑袜并使用助行器。”

options.en: “If I feel embarrassed asking for help, I will move quickly so I do not bother anyone.”
options.zh: “如果我不好意思求助，我会快点走，免得麻烦别人。”

options.en: “I will keep my phone, water, and call light where I can reach them.”
options.zh: “我会把手机、水和呼叫铃放在伸手可及的地方。”

questions.topic: Pressure injury prevention in long-term care

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items.
stem.zh: 查看患者病历，并回答病例题。

correct.en: This case emphasizes pressure-injury prevention by connecting risk cues to individualized pressure relief, moisture management, nutrition support, delegation boundaries, and outcome evaluation.
correct.zh: 本病例强调通过将风险线索与个体化减压、潮湿管理、营养支持、委托边界和结果评估相连接来预防压力性损伤。

testTakingStrategy.en: Pressure-injury questions are usually safer when you target risk drivers rather than memorize one turning interval for every client.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 压力性损伤题通常应针对风险来源，而不是死记每位患者都用同一个翻身间隔。

glossary.defZh: 皮肤或深部组织因压力或剪切力受损
通过体位或器具减少局部压力
尿液、粪便、汗液或渗液长期接触造成的皮肤炎症或破损
帮助分散身体压力的床垫、坐垫或覆盖层

title.en: Case study: pressure injury prevention in long-term care
title.zh: 病例：长期照护中的压力性损伤预防

summary.en: A resident in long-term care has multiple pressure-injury risk factors. The nurse updates the prevention plan after a skin-risk huddle.
summary.zh: 一名长期照护住民存在多项压力性损伤风险因素。护士在皮肤风险小组讨论后更新预防计划。

content.en:
Resident is 86 years old, admitted for long-term care after functional decline. Uses wheelchair for meals and needs moderate assistance to turn in bed.
Sacral skin has blanchable redness after sitting for breakfast. Both heels rest directly on mattress when supine.
Brief was wet at 0700; loose stool noted near sacrum. Resident reports, “My bottom burns sometimes.”
Ate 25% of meals yesterday and drank little fluid.
content.zh:
住民86岁，因功能下降入住长期照护。用轮椅进餐，在床上翻身需中等协助。
早餐后骶尾部皮肤出现可褪色红斑。仰卧时双足跟直接压在床垫上。
07:00尿布潮湿；骶尾部附近有稀便。住民说：“我屁股有时灼痛。”
昨日进食约25%，饮水少。

content.en:
Moisture-barrier product in use after each incontinence episode. Heels are offloaded. Staff document repositioning and comfort checks based on the individualized plan.
Sacral redness resolves after pressure relief; skin remains intact. Resident reports less burning. Intake improved to about 50% of meals with supplements offered.
content.zh:
每次失禁后使用皮肤屏障产品。足跟已减压。工作人员按个体化计划记录重新摆位和舒适度检查。
骶尾部红斑减压后消退；皮肤仍完整。住民报告灼痛减轻。提供营养补充后进食改善至约50%。

questions.topic: Pressure injury prevention in long-term care

stem.en: For each finding, select the prevention concern it most directly supports.
stem.zh: 针对每项发现，选择其最直接支持的预防关注点。

correct.en: Immobility and heel pressure require offloading and repositioning; moisture requires incontinence care and barrier protection; low intake requires nutrition/hydration support; blanchable redness requires pressure relief and monitoring, not staging as an open injury.
correct.zh: 活动受限和足跟受压需要减压和重新摆位；潮湿需要失禁护理和屏障保护；摄入不足需要营养/水分支持；可褪色红斑需要减压和监测，不应分期为开放性损伤。

byChoice.en: Limited independent turning is an immobility risk requiring a repositioning plan.
byChoice.zh: 不能独立翻身是活动受限风险，需要重新摆位计划。

byChoice.en: Moist briefs and stool exposure primarily increase moisture and skin-breakdown risk.
byChoice.zh: 潮湿尿布和粪便接触主要增加潮湿和皮肤破损风险。

byChoice.en: Low meal and fluid intake supports nutrition/hydration risk.
byChoice.zh: 进食和饮水不足支持营养/水分风险。

byChoice.en: Heels resting on the mattress indicate a pressure/offloading concern.
byChoice.zh: 足跟压在床垫上提示压力/减压问题。

byChoice.en: Blanchable redness is an early warning sign that requires pressure relief and monitoring.
byChoice.zh: 可褪色红斑是早期警示信号，需要减压和监测。

testTakingStrategy.en: Pressure-injury prevention starts by naming the risk driver: pressure, shear, moisture, nutrition, or mobility.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 压力性损伤预防要先说清风险来源：压力、剪切力、潮湿、营养或活动能力。

glossary.defZh: 按压后颜色变浅的皮肤发红，提示受压但不等同于1期损伤
用体位或器具减轻局部组织压力

rows.en: Resident cannot reposition independently in bed.
rows.zh: 住民不能在床上独立变换体位。

rows.en: Brief is wet; loose stool noted near sacrum.
rows.zh: 尿布潮湿；骶尾部附近有稀便。

rows.en: Ate 25% of meals yesterday and drank little fluid.
rows.zh: 昨日进食约25%，饮水少。

rows.en: Both heels rest directly on the mattress.
rows.zh: 双足跟直接压在床垫上。

rows.en: Sacral redness blanches when gently pressed.
rows.zh: 轻压骶尾部发红处可褪色。

questions.topic: Pressure injury prevention in long-term care

stem.en: Which interventions should the nurse include in the prevention plan? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 护士应将哪些干预纳入预防计划？选择所有适用项。

correct.en: The plan should individualize repositioning, offload heels, manage moisture promptly, use a pressure-redistribution surface as indicated, and consult dietary support. Massage, donut cushions, and delaying incontinence care increase harm.
correct.zh: 计划应个体化重新摆位、足跟减压、及时管理潮湿、按需使用减压支撑面并咨询营养支持。按摩、甜甜圈坐垫和延迟失禁护理会增加伤害。

byChoice.en: Floating/offloading heels reduces pressure on a common high-risk site.
byChoice.zh: 悬空/减压足跟可减少常见高风险部位的压力。

byChoice.en: Massaging reddened bony areas can damage tissue and should be avoided.
byChoice.zh: 按摩发红骨突处可能损伤组织，应避免。

byChoice.en: A pressure-redistribution surface reduces sustained pressure for an immobile resident.
byChoice.zh: 减压支撑面可减少活动受限住民的持续压力。

byChoice.en: A dietitian can address poor intake and protein/calorie needs.
byChoice.zh: 营养师可处理摄入不足和蛋白质/热量需求。

byChoice.en: Prompt incontinence care and barrier cream reduce moisture-associated skin damage.
byChoice.zh: 及时失禁护理和屏障霜可减少潮湿相关皮肤损伤。

byChoice.en: A repositioning schedule should be individualized based on skin tolerance, mobility, support surface, and resident comfort.
byChoice.zh: 重新摆位计划应根据皮肤耐受性、活动能力、支撑面和住民舒适度个体化。

testTakingStrategy.en: Choose interventions that reduce pressure, shear, moisture, and nutrition risk without damaging tissue.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 选择能降低压力、剪切力、潮湿和营养风险且不会损伤组织的干预。

glossary.defZh: 帮助分散身体压力的床垫或垫面
保护皮肤免受尿液、粪便或湿气刺激的产品

options.en: Offload the heels so they do not rest directly on the mattress.
options.zh: 足跟减压，使其不直接压在床垫上。

options.en: Massage reddened areas over bony prominences each shift.
options.zh: 每班按摩骨突处发红区域。

options.en: Request evaluation for a pressure-redistribution mattress or overlay.
options.zh: 申请评估是否需要减压床垫或床垫覆盖层。

options.en: Consult the dietitian for poor intake and protein/calorie needs.
options.zh: 因摄入不足和蛋白质/热量需求咨询营养师。

options.en: Provide prompt incontinence care and apply moisture-barrier product as ordered or per protocol.
options.zh: 及时进行失禁护理，并按医嘱或流程使用皮肤屏障产品。

options.en: Create an individualized repositioning schedule based on skin tolerance, support surface, and mobility.
options.zh: 根据皮肤耐受性、支撑面和活动能力制定个体化重新摆位计划。

questions.topic: Pressure injury prevention in long-term care

stem.en: Which task is appropriate for the nurse to delegate to assistive personnel for this resident?
stem.zh: 对于该住民，护士可将哪项任务适当委托给辅助人员？

correct.en: Assistive personnel can perform routine repositioning and report skin changes after the nurse establishes the plan. The nurse retains responsibility for assessment, staging, care-plan revision, and clinical referrals.
correct.zh: 在护士制定计划后，辅助人员可执行常规重新摆位并报告皮肤变化。护士仍负责评估、分期、修改护理计划和临床转介。

byChoice.en: Staging or diagnosing a pressure injury requires nursing assessment and is not delegated.
byChoice.zh: 压力性损伤分期或诊断需要护士评估，不能委托。

byChoice.en: This is appropriate delegation: a routine care activity with instructions to report changes.
byChoice.zh: 这是适当委托：常规护理活动，并要求报告变化。

byChoice.en: Selecting a support surface involves assessment and coordination, not independent assistive-personnel decision making.
byChoice.zh: 选择支撑面涉及评估和协调，不能由辅助人员独立决定。

byChoice.en: Changing the care plan requires nursing judgment.
byChoice.zh: 修改护理计划需要护士判断。

testTakingStrategy.en: Delegate tasks, not assessment or care-plan judgment.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 委托的是任务，而不是评估或护理计划判断。

glossary.defZh: 在护士监督下执行基本护理任务的人员

options.en: Determine whether the sacral redness is a stage 1 pressure injury.
options.zh: 判断骶尾部发红是否为1期压力性损伤。

options.en: Reposition the resident according to the posted plan and report any new redness or pain.
options.zh: 按张贴的计划帮助住民重新摆位，并报告任何新的发红或疼痛。

options.en: Choose which pressure-redistribution surface the resident should receive.
options.zh: 决定住民应使用哪种减压支撑面。

options.en: Revise the repositioning frequency based on the resident's skin assessment.
options.zh: 根据住民皮肤评估修改重新摆位频率。

questions.topic: Pressure injury prevention in long-term care

stem.en: Use the 48-hour update to evaluate the plan.
stem.zh: 根据48小时更新评估计划。

correct.en: Less moisture exposure and redness that resolves after pressure relief suggest the prevention plan is improving skin tolerance. Nonblanchable redness, open skin, or worsening pain would require prompt reassessment.
correct.zh: 潮湿暴露减少且减压后红斑消退，提示预防计划改善了皮肤耐受性。不可褪色红斑、皮肤破损或疼痛加重则需要及时重新评估。

byChoice.en: Improving skin tolerance is correct because the update shows redness resolves with pressure relief and moisture is controlled.
byChoice.zh: 皮肤耐受性改善正确，因为更新显示红斑减压后消退且潮湿得到控制。

byChoice.en: Continue and reassess is correct; the plan is not abandoned, but the nurse keeps monitoring high-risk skin.
byChoice.zh: 继续并重新评估正确；不应放弃计划，但护士需持续监测高风险皮肤。

testTakingStrategy.en: Evaluate outcomes by comparing the newest skin findings with the original risk cues.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 评估结果时，将最新皮肤发现与原始风险线索比较。

glossary.defZh: 按压后不变白的发红，提示组织受压损伤风险更高

clozeStem.en: The 48-hour update suggests {{1}}, so the nurse should {{2}}.

options.en: improving skin tolerance
options.zh: 皮肤耐受性改善

options.en: confirmed wound infection
options.zh: 已确认伤口感染

options.en: need for strict bedrest
options.zh: 需要严格卧床

options.en: continue the prevention plan and reassess skin each shift
options.zh: 继续预防计划并每班重新评估皮肤

options.en: stop repositioning because redness has improved
options.zh: 因发红改善而停止重新摆位

options.en: massage the sacrum to increase circulation
options.zh: 按摩骶尾部以增加循环

questions.topic: Discharge teaching with limited English proficiency

stem.en: A nurse is preparing discharge teaching for a client who speaks limited English and will manage a new low-sodium diet at home. Place the nursing actions in the best order.
stem.zh: 护士准备为一名英语能力有限、出院后需要在家执行低钠饮食的患者进行出院宣教。请将护理措施按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Use plain-language instructions and show the client how to read sodium amounts on a food label.
options.zh: 使用通俗语言说明，并示范如何查看食品标签上的钠含量。

options.en: Determine the client's preferred language and ask what written format is easiest to use.
options.zh: 确认患者偏好的语言，并询问哪种书面格式最容易使用。

options.en: Arrange for a qualified medical interpreter before giving diet instructions.
options.zh: 在进行饮食指导前安排合格的医疗口译员。

options.en: Ask the client to explain in their own words how they will choose lower-sodium foods at home.
options.zh: 请患者用自己的话说明在家如何选择低钠食物。

options.en: Document the teaching method, interpreter use, and any remaining learning needs.
options.zh: 记录宣教方法、口译员使用情况以及仍需学习的内容。

correct.en: The nurse first assesses language and literacy needs, then uses a qualified interpreter, teaches with plain language and demonstration, verifies understanding with teach-back, and documents the outcome and remaining needs.
correct.zh: 护士应先评估语言与读写需求，然后使用合格口译员，以通俗语言和示范进行宣教，再用回授法确认理解，最后记录结果和仍需学习的内容。

byChoice.en: Plain language and label demonstration are appropriate after communication support is in place.
byChoice.zh: 在沟通支持到位后，使用通俗语言和标签示范是合适的。

byChoice.en: Assessment comes first. The nurse needs to know the client's preferred language and usable format before planning teaching.
byChoice.zh: 评估应最先进行。护士在制定宣教方式前，需要了解患者偏好的语言和可使用的资料格式。

byChoice.en: A qualified medical interpreter should be arranged before instructions are given so teaching is accurate and confidential.
byChoice.zh: 在进行指导前应安排合格医疗口译员，以确保宣教准确并保护隐私。

byChoice.en: Teach-back follows teaching and checks whether the explanation was clear enough for the client to apply it at home.
byChoice.zh: 回授法应在宣教后进行，用来确认说明是否足够清楚，患者能否在家应用。

byChoice.en: Documentation is completed after assessment, teaching, and evaluation so the record reflects what occurred and what remains unresolved.
byChoice.zh: 记录应在评估、宣教和评价后完成，才能反映已完成的内容和未解决的需求。

testTakingStrategy.en: For discharge-teaching sequences, use the nursing process: assess, support communication, teach, evaluate understanding, then document.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 遇到出院宣教排序题，按护理程序思考：先评估，再支持沟通，然后宣教、评价理解，最后记录。

glossary.defZh: 经过训练、能准确传达医疗信息并维护隐私的口译人员。
让患者用自己的话复述或演示要做的事项，以确认理解。
理解和使用健康信息以做出照护决定的能力。

questions.topic: Caregiver strain and family coping in dementia

stem.en: A spouse caring for a client with dementia says, "I have not slept more than 3 hours a night, and yesterday I yelled because I could not take it anymore." The client is clean and uninjured. Place the nurse's responses in the best order.
stem.zh: 一名照护失智症患者的配偶说："我每晚睡不到3小时，昨天我忍不住大吼，因为真的撑不下去了。"患者清洁且没有受伤。请将护士的回应按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Create a written crisis plan with contacts to use if the caregiver feels unsafe or unable to continue care.
options.zh: 制定书面的危机计划，列出照护者感到不安全或无法继续照护时可联系的对象。

options.en: Assess immediate safety, including whether the caregiver fears harming self or the client.
options.zh: 评估当前安全，包括照护者是否担心会伤害自己或患者。

options.en: Ask what support the family currently has and what tasks feel most overwhelming.
options.zh: 询问家庭目前有哪些支持，以及哪些照护任务最让人难以承受。

options.en: Collaborate with the case manager or social worker to arrange respite and dementia-care resources.
options.zh: 与个案管理师或社工合作，安排喘息服务和失智照护资源。

options.en: Acknowledge the caregiver's exhaustion without judgment.
options.zh: 不带评判地肯定照护者的疲惫感。

correct.en: Safety is assessed first because caregiver exhaustion can escalate to harm. The nurse then validates feelings, gathers more data about supports and burdens, activates resources, and leaves the family with a concrete crisis plan.
correct.zh: 由于照护者过度疲惫可能升级为伤害风险，首先评估安全。随后护士应接纳其感受，进一步了解支持和负担，启动资源，并为家庭留下具体危机计划。

byChoice.en: A written crisis plan reinforces safety and tells the caregiver exactly what to do if the situation worsens.
byChoice.zh: 书面危机计划可强化安全，并明确告诉照护者情况恶化时该怎么做。

byChoice.en: Immediate safety assessment is the priority when the caregiver describes loss of control, even if no injury is present.
byChoice.zh: 当照护者描述失控时，即使目前没有伤害，也应优先评估即时安全。

byChoice.en: Assessing current support and the most stressful tasks helps match interventions to the family's actual needs.
byChoice.zh: 评估现有支持和压力最大的任务，有助于将干预与家庭实际需求相匹配。

byChoice.en: Respite and dementia resources are appropriate after the nurse understands the caregiver's needs and risks.
byChoice.zh: 在护士了解照护者需求和风险后，安排喘息服务和失智资源是合适的。

byChoice.en: Validation reduces shame and supports therapeutic communication after urgent safety concerns are addressed.
byChoice.zh: 在处理紧急安全问题后，肯定其感受可减少羞耻感，并建立治疗性沟通。

testTakingStrategy.en: When emotional distress includes possible loss of control, assess safety before offering reassurance or routine referrals.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当情绪困扰伴随可能失控时，应先评估安全，再提供安慰或常规转介。

glossary.defZh: 长期照护导致的身体、情绪、经济或社会负担。
让主要照护者暂时休息的短期替代照护服务。
当安全或照护能力恶化时可立即采取的联系和行动步骤。

questions.topic: Wheelchair-to-bed transfer in rehabilitation

stem.en: A client in rehabilitation can bear partial weight on the right leg and is ready to transfer from a wheelchair to the bed with one-person assist. Place the nursing actions in the safest order.
stem.zh: 一名康复期患者右腿可部分负重，准备在一名工作人员协助下从轮椅转移到床上。请将护理措施按最安全顺序排列。

options.en: Lock the wheelchair brakes, move footrests out of the way, and ensure the client is wearing nonskid footwear.
options.zh: 锁定轮椅刹车，移开脚踏板，并确认患者穿防滑鞋。

options.en: Position the wheelchair at a slight angle next to the bed on the client's stronger side.
options.zh: 将轮椅以轻微角度放在床旁、靠近患者较有力的一侧。

options.en: Apply the gait belt snugly around the client's waist while the client is sitting upright.
options.zh: 在患者坐直时，将步态带贴合地系在腰部。

options.en: Pivot toward the bed, help the client sit, and check alignment before removing the gait belt.
options.zh: 向床的方向转身，协助患者坐下，并在取下步态带前检查身体对齐。

options.en: Ask the client to push from the wheelchair armrests while the nurse stabilizes the gait belt.
options.zh: 请患者从轮椅扶手用力撑起，同时护士稳定步态带。

correct.en: Safe transfer begins with positioning equipment, then securing the environment and footwear. The gait belt is applied while the client is sitting upright, the client pushes from the armrests rather than pulling on the nurse, and the nurse assists the pivot and sitting alignment before removing support.
correct.zh: 安全转移先从设备摆位开始，再固定环境并确保防滑鞋。步态带应在患者坐直时系好，患者应从扶手撑起而不是拉护士，护士协助转身和坐下对齐后再取下支撑。

byChoice.en: Brakes, footrests, and nonskid footwear reduce fall and trip risk before the client stands.
byChoice.zh: 在患者站起前，刹车、脚踏板和防滑鞋可降低跌倒和绊倒风险。

byChoice.en: The wheelchair and bed must be positioned before brakes, footrests, and the actual transfer are addressed.
byChoice.zh: 在处理刹车、脚踏板和实际转移前，必须先摆好轮椅和床的位置。

byChoice.en: A gait belt is secured while the client is upright and before standing so the nurse can assist safely.
byChoice.zh: 步态带应在患者坐直、站起前系好，使护士能安全协助。

byChoice.en: The pivot and sitting alignment occur last; support should not be removed until the client is safely seated.
byChoice.zh: 转身和坐下对齐是最后步骤；在患者安全坐稳前不应移除支撑。

byChoice.en: Pushing from armrests uses the client's strength and avoids pulling on the nurse, which can destabilize both people.
byChoice.zh: 从扶手撑起可利用患者自身力量，避免拉扯护士导致双方不稳。

testTakingStrategy.en: For transfer questions, secure the environment before the client stands and remove support only after the client is safely positioned.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 遇到转移题，先固定环境再让患者站立；只有患者安全就位后才移除支撑。

glossary.defZh: 系在腰部帮助工作人员安全协助站立、转移或行走的带子。
患肢只能承受部分体重，需要按医嘱限制承重量。
站起后以小范围转身将身体从一个表面转到另一个表面。

questions.topic: Moisture management for pressure injury prevention

stem.en: An older adult in a rehabilitation facility is incontinent of urine, spends most of the day in bed, and has intact but reddened sacral skin that blanches. Place the nurse's preventive actions in the best order for this shift.
stem.zh: 一名康复机构中的老年患者有尿失禁，大部分时间卧床，骶尾部皮肤完整但发红且可褪色。请将本班次护士的预防措施按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Update the care plan with the turning schedule and skin findings to report.
options.zh: 更新护理计划，写明翻身时间表和需要报告的皮肤发现。

options.en: Cleanse the skin promptly after incontinence using a gentle cleanser.
options.zh: 失禁后立即使用温和清洁剂清洁皮肤。

options.en: Apply a moisture-barrier product to areas exposed to urine.
options.zh: 在暴露于尿液的部位涂抹防潮屏障产品。

options.en: Assess the sacrum, heels, and skin folds and note whether redness blanches.
options.zh: 评估骶尾部、足跟和皮肤皱褶处，并记录红斑是否可褪色。

options.en: Reposition using pillows or wedges to offload the sacrum and heels.
options.zh: 使用枕头或楔形垫重新摆位，以减轻骶尾部和足跟受压。

correct.en: The nurse assesses first, then removes moisture and protects skin, then offloads pressure, and finally updates the care plan so prevention is consistent across caregivers and shifts.
correct.zh: 护士先评估，然后清除潮湿并保护皮肤，再减轻压力，最后更新护理计划，使不同照护者和班次都能持续预防。

byChoice.en: The care plan is updated after the nurse knows the skin findings and interventions used.
byChoice.zh: 护士明确皮肤发现和已采取的干预后，再更新护理计划。

byChoice.en: Urine and moisture should be removed before barrier products are applied.
byChoice.zh: 在涂抹屏障产品前，应先去除尿液和潮湿。

byChoice.en: A moisture barrier helps protect intact skin from ongoing exposure after cleansing.
byChoice.zh: 清洁后使用防潮屏障可保护完整皮肤免受持续刺激。

byChoice.en: Assessment determines whether the reddened area is still blanchable and identifies other high-risk areas before interventions begin.
byChoice.zh: 评估可判断发红区域是否仍可褪色，并在干预前找出其他高风险部位。

byChoice.en: Repositioning and offloading reduce pressure and shear after skin moisture is addressed.
byChoice.zh: 处理皮肤潮湿后，通过重新摆位和减压可减少压力和剪切力。

testTakingStrategy.en: For skin-prevention sequences, assess first, remove the cause of irritation, protect the skin, reduce pressure, and communicate the plan.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 皮肤预防排序题可按：先评估、去除刺激原因、保护皮肤、减轻压力、沟通计划。

glossary.defZh: 按压后暂时变白的红斑，提示皮肤受压但尚未达到不可褪色损伤。
用于保护皮肤免受尿液、粪便或潮湿刺激的产品。
通过摆位或支撑设备减少某一部位承受的压力。

questions.topic: Community resource referral before discharge

stem.en: A client with limited income is being discharged after rehabilitation. The client says, "I missed therapy appointments before because I had no ride, and some weeks I run out of food." Place the nurse's actions in the best order.
stem.zh: 一名收入有限的患者康复后准备出院。患者说："我以前因为没有车去而错过治疗预约，有些星期还会没饭吃。"请将护士的措施按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Ask permission to share the client's needs with the discharge planner or social worker.
options.zh: 征得患者同意，将其需求告知出院计划人员或社工。

options.en: Assess the client's transportation, food access, insurance, and preferred contact method.
options.zh: 评估患者的交通、食物获取、保险情况和偏好的联系方式。

options.en: Collaborate to arrange referrals for transportation assistance and food support programs.
options.zh: 合作安排交通协助和食物支持项目的转介。

options.en: Schedule or confirm follow-up appointments at times the client can realistically attend.
options.zh: 安排或确认患者实际能够参加的随访预约时间。

options.en: Ask the client to state the first contact they will use if transportation or food access fails.
options.zh: 请患者说出如果交通或食物获取再次出现问题时首先联系谁。

correct.en: The nurse first completes a focused social-needs assessment, obtains permission to involve the appropriate team member, arranges targeted resources, confirms realistic follow-up, and uses teach-back to verify the client knows what to do if barriers recur.
correct.zh: 护士应先进行针对性的社会需求评估，征得同意后联系合适团队成员，安排相应资源，确认实际可行的随访，并用回授法确认患者知道障碍再次出现时该怎么做。

byChoice.en: Consent and privacy should be addressed before sharing personal needs with other team members or agencies.
byChoice.zh: 在向团队其他成员或机构分享个人需求前，应处理同意和隐私问题。

byChoice.en: Specific barriers must be assessed before referrals are chosen.
byChoice.zh: 在选择转介前，必须先评估具体障碍。

byChoice.en: Resource referrals are made after the nurse knows the needs and has permission to coordinate care.
byChoice.zh: 在明确需求并获得协调照护的同意后，才能进行资源转介。

byChoice.en: Follow-up is more likely to succeed when appointment timing matches available transportation and support.
byChoice.zh: 预约时间与可用交通和支持相匹配时，随访更可能成功。

byChoice.en: Teach-back at the end confirms the client has a usable contingency plan.
byChoice.zh: 最后使用回授法可确认患者有可执行的备用计划。

testTakingStrategy.en: For discharge barriers, assess the exact need, protect privacy, involve the correct resource, then verify the plan is usable.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 遇到出院障碍题，先评估具体需求，保护隐私，联系正确资源，再确认计划可实际执行。

glossary.defZh: 协助安排出院后服务、设备、随访和资源的团队成员。
无法稳定获得足够、合适食物的情况。
帮助患者前往医疗预约或服务地点的社区或保险相关支持。

questions.topic: Breast cancer screening teaching

stem.en: A 41-year-old client at average risk for breast cancer asks when routine screening mammograms should begin and how often they are generally recommended. Complete the teaching statement.
stem.zh: 一名41岁、乳腺癌平均风险的患者询问常规筛查乳房X线摄影应从何时开始，以及通常建议多久一次。请完成宣教语句。

prompt.en: Begin routine screening mammograms at age ___ years.
prompt.zh: 常规筛查乳房X线摄影从 ___ 岁开始。

prompt.en: For average-risk adults in the recommended age range, screening is generally every ___ years.
prompt.zh: 对于推荐年龄范围内的平均风险成人，通常每 ___ 年筛查一次。

correct.en: For average-risk women, current USPSTF guidance recommends screening mammography beginning at age 40 and continuing every 2 years through age 74. Individual risk factors may require provider-specific recommendations.
correct.zh: 对于平均风险女性，当前USPSTF建议从40岁开始进行筛查乳房X线摄影，并至74岁每2年一次。若有个体风险因素，可能需要根据医嘱调整。

byChoice.en: Age 40 is the recommended starting age for average-risk routine mammography screening.
byChoice.zh: 40岁是平均风险人群常规乳房X线筛查的建议起始年龄。

byChoice.en: Every 2 years is the general recommended interval for average-risk adults in the screening age range.
byChoice.zh: 每2年一次是筛查年龄范围内平均风险成人的一般建议间隔。

testTakingStrategy.en: In screening questions, identify whether the client is average risk or high risk before applying routine age-based recommendations.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 筛查题要先判断患者是平均风险还是高风险，再套用常规按年龄的建议。

glossary.defZh: 用于筛查或评估乳腺异常的低剂量X线检查。
没有已知高风险因素时的一般风险水平。
每2年发生或进行一次。

questions.topic: Lung cancer screening eligibility

stem.en: The nurse is reviewing lung cancer screening eligibility with an adult who smoked for many years. Complete the eligibility teaching statement for routine low-dose CT screening.
stem.zh: 护士正在与一名多年吸烟史成人复习肺癌筛查资格。请完成关于常规低剂量CT筛查资格的宣教语句。

prompt.en: Eligible adults are generally ages ___ to 80 years.
prompt.zh: 通常符合条件的成人年龄为 ___ 至80岁。

prompt.en: They have at least ___ pack-years of smoking history.
prompt.zh: 其吸烟史至少为 ___ 包年。

prompt.en: If no longer smoking, they quit within the past ___ years.
prompt.zh: 如果已戒烟，应是在过去 ___ 年内戒烟。

correct.en: Routine annual low-dose CT screening is recommended for adults ages 50 to 80 who have a 20 pack-year smoking history and currently smoke or quit within the past 15 years. Screening decisions should also consider health status and ability to undergo curative treatment.
correct.zh: 常规每年低剂量CT筛查建议用于50至80岁、有20包年吸烟史且目前仍吸烟或过去15年内戒烟的成人。筛查决定还应考虑健康状况和是否能接受根治性治疗。

byChoice.en: Age 50 is the lower age threshold in the routine eligibility range.
byChoice.zh: 50岁是常规资格年龄范围的下限。

byChoice.en: A 20 pack-year history is the smoking exposure threshold for routine screening eligibility.
byChoice.zh: 20包年吸烟史是常规筛查资格的吸烟暴露阈值。

byChoice.en: Former smokers remain eligible if they quit within the past 15 years.
byChoice.zh: 既往吸烟者如果在过去15年内戒烟，仍可能符合筛查条件。

testTakingStrategy.en: For screening eligibility, look for all required criteria; one missing criterion can make the routine recommendation inapplicable.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 筛查资格题要找齐所有必要条件；缺少一个条件，常规建议可能就不适用。

glossary.defZh: 使用较低辐射剂量进行的CT检查，可用于肺癌筛查。
每天吸烟包数乘以吸烟年数，用于估计吸烟暴露量。
根据年龄、风险因素和健康状况判断是否适合接受筛查。

questions.topic: Repositioning schedule for pressure injury prevention

stem.en: A bedbound client is at risk for pressure injury. The facility care plan states the client should be turned at least every 2 hours while in bed unless an individualized plan requires a different interval. Complete the teaching statement for the family caregiver.
stem.zh: 一名卧床患者有压力性损伤风险。机构护理计划规定，除非个体化计划要求不同间隔，患者卧床时至少每2小时翻身一次。请完成给家庭照护者的宣教语句。

prompt.en: Reposition the client at least every ___ hours while in bed, following the care plan.
prompt.zh: 按照护理计划，患者卧床时至少每 ___ 小时重新摆位一次。

correct.en: The care plan specifies repositioning at least every 2 hours while the client is in bed. The nurse should also teach that the schedule may be individualized based on skin tolerance, support surfaces, comfort, and clinical condition.
correct.zh: 护理计划规定患者卧床时至少每2小时重新摆位一次。护士还应说明，翻身时间表可根据皮肤耐受性、支撑面、舒适度和临床状况进行个体化调整。

byChoice.en: Two hours matches the stated care plan and is a common prevention interval, while the individualized plan still takes priority.
byChoice.zh: 2小时与题干中的护理计划相符，也是常用预防间隔，但个体化计划仍优先。

testTakingStrategy.en: When a stem gives a facility plan or provider order, answer from that plan unless the action is unsafe.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当题干给出机构计划或医嘱时，除非该措施不安全，应按该计划作答。

glossary.defZh: 因疾病、虚弱或功能限制大部分时间需要留在床上。
改变身体姿势以减轻压力、改善舒适度并保护皮肤。
床垫、坐垫等帮助分散压力的设备或材料。

questions.topic: Chronic illness self-management foot inspection

stem.en: A client with diabetes and decreased foot sensation is learning home self-management. Complete the statement that shows the client understands foot-care teaching.
stem.zh: 一名糖尿病且足部感觉减退的患者正在学习居家自我管理。请完成显示患者理解足部护理宣教的语句。

prompt.zh: 我会 ___ 检查我的双脚。

correct.en: Decreased sensation can prevent the client from noticing injury. Daily foot inspection helps identify redness, blisters, cuts, or pressure areas early so the client can seek care before complications develop.
correct.zh: 感觉减退会使患者不易发现损伤。每日检查足部有助于早期发现发红、水疱、割伤或受压部位，以便并发症发生前及时求医。

byChoice.en: Daily or every day is the expected frequency for self-inspection when foot sensation is decreased.
byChoice.zh: 当足部感觉减退时，预期的自我检查频率是每天。

testTakingStrategy.en: For chronic illness self-management, choose actions that let the client detect problems early and act before complications worsen.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 慢性病自我管理题应选择能让患者早期发现问题并在并发症恶化前采取行动的措施。

glossary.defZh: 触觉、痛觉或温度觉下降，可能使损伤不易被发现。
患者在日常生活中执行监测、预防和照护措施。
查看足底、趾间和足跟是否有发红、破损、水疱或压力点。

questions.topic: Community resources for older adult support

stem.en: An older adult is being discharged home and needs help finding local meal delivery, caregiver support, transportation, and benefits counseling. Complete the nurse's referral statement.
stem.zh: 一名老年患者即将出院回家，需要帮助寻找当地送餐、照护者支持、交通和福利咨询。请完成护士的转介说明。

prompt.en: I will ask the case manager to connect you with the local ___.
prompt.zh: 我会请个案管理师帮您联系当地的 ___。

correct.en: An Area Agency on Aging is an appropriate community resource for older adults and caregivers who need help locating services such as meals, transportation, caregiver support, and benefits counseling. The nurse should involve case management or social work for referral coordination.
correct.zh: 老龄事务地区机构是适合老年人和照护者的社区资源，可帮助寻找送餐、交通、照护者支持和福利咨询等服务。护士应让个案管理或社工协助转介协调。

byChoice.en: The Area Agency on Aging is the best fit because the client's needs span multiple community-based aging services rather than a single medical appointment.
byChoice.zh: 老龄事务地区机构最合适，因为患者的需求涉及多种社区老年服务，而不只是单一医疗预约。

testTakingStrategy.en: When needs are nonmedical but affect safe discharge, think case management, social work, and community agencies rather than adding more bedside teaching alone.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当需求不是单纯医疗问题但影响安全出院时，应想到个案管理、社工和社区机构，而不是只增加床旁宣教。

glossary.defZh: 帮助老年人和照护者连接当地服务与福利资源的社区机构。
帮助了解保险、补助、餐食、交通或照护服务资格的咨询。
协助患者与合适服务或机构建立联系并完成后续安排。

questions.topic: Caregiver burden after dementia discharge

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items.
stem.zh: 查看患者病历，并回答病例题。

correct.en: This case centers on recognizing caregiver role strain and dementia-related home-safety risks, using therapeutic communication, and planning interdisciplinary community supports before unsafe caregiving patterns worsen.
correct.zh: 本病例重点是识别照护者角色压力和痴呆相关居家安全风险，使用治疗性沟通，并在不安全照护模式恶化前规划跨专业社区支持。

testTakingStrategy.en: When the family caregiver is the safety system, the nurse must assess the caregiver's capacity as carefully as the client's symptoms.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当家庭照护者就是安全系统时，护士必须像评估患者症状一样仔细评估照护者能力。

glossary.defZh: 照护任务造成的身体、情绪、经济或社会压力
为主要照护者提供短期替代照护和休息的服务
为认知障碍患者预防走失并在走失时快速应对的计划
请对方用自己的话说明计划以确认理解

title.en: Case study: dementia discharge and caregiver burden
title.zh: 病例：痴呆出院后的照护者负担

summary.en: An older adult with dementia was discharged home with the daughter as the primary caregiver. The home-health nurse makes the first follow-up call and visit.
summary.zh: 一名患有痴呆的老年人出院回家，由女儿担任主要照护者。居家护理护士进行首次随访电话和家访。

content.en:
Client: 79-year-old with moderate dementia discharged 5 days ago after observation for dehydration. Lives with adult daughter, who works remotely.
Daughter reports the client has slept poorly, wanders toward the front door after dinner, and asks repeatedly to “go home.”
Two morning medication doses were found in a coffee cup. The daughter says she slept about 3 hours most nights this week.
Yesterday the client turned on the stove while the daughter was showering. No injury occurred.
content.zh:
患者：79岁，中度痴呆，5天前因脱水观察后出院。与成年女儿同住，女儿远程工作。
女儿报告患者睡眠差，晚饭后会走向前门，并反复说“要回家”。
在咖啡杯中发现两次早晨药物未服。女儿说本周多数夜晚约睡3小时。
昨天女儿洗澡时，患者打开了炉灶，未造成伤害。

content.en:
Daughter tearfully states, “I promised I would never put Mom in a facility, but I do not know how much longer I can do this.”
Current services: primary care follow-up in 1 week; memory clinic in 2 weeks. No home-health aide, respite care, or adult day program is arranged. Daughter has not told other family members how exhausted she feels.
content.zh:
女儿流泪说：“我答应过永远不把妈妈送进机构，但我不知道自己还能这样撑多久。”
当前服务：1周后初级保健复诊；2周后记忆门诊。尚未安排居家护理员、喘息照护或成人日间项目。女儿尚未告诉其他家人自己有多疲惫。

questions.topic: Caregiver burden after dementia discharge

stem.en: Classify each cue from the record.
stem.zh: 对病历中的每条线索进行分类。

correct.en: Missed caregiver sleep, medication confusion, wandering, and the caregiver's tearful statement are not normal adjustment alone; they show escalating strain and safety risk. The intact follow-up appointment is a protective factor.
correct.zh: 照护者睡眠不足、用药混乱、走失以及照护者流泪的表达，并不只是普通适应过程；这些提示照护压力加重和安全风险。后续门诊预约已安排是保护性因素。

byChoice.en: Sleeping 3 hours most nights is a caregiver-strain cue because impaired rest reduces safe caregiving capacity.
byChoice.zh: 多数夜晚只睡3小时提示照护者压力，因为休息不足会降低安全照护能力。

byChoice.en: The stove incident is an immediate home-safety risk linked to impaired supervision and dementia-related wandering.
byChoice.zh: 炉灶事件是与监督不足及痴呆相关走失有关的即时居家安全风险。

byChoice.en: A scheduled memory-clinic appointment supports continuity of care rather than indicating acute caregiver overload.
byChoice.zh: 已预约记忆门诊支持照护连续性，并不表示急性照护者负担。

byChoice.en: Medication doses found in a cup suggest an unsafe medication-management barrier.
byChoice.zh: 在杯子里发现未服药物提示用药管理存在不安全障碍。

byChoice.en: The statement about being unable to keep doing this is a direct caregiver role-strain cue that requires further assessment.
byChoice.zh: “我撑不下去了”是直接的照护者角色压力线索，需要进一步评估。

testTakingStrategy.en: First separate protective factors from risk cues, then decide which risks threaten immediate safety or caregiver capacity.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 先把保护性因素和风险线索分开，再判断哪些风险威胁即时安全或照护能力。

glossary.defZh: 长期照护责任造成身心负担并影响照护能力的状态
认知障碍患者无目的或不安全地离开安全区域

rows.en: Daughter reports sleeping 3 hours most nights since discharge.
rows.zh: 女儿说出院后多数夜晚只睡3小时。

rows.en: Client left the stove on while the daughter was showering.
rows.zh: 女儿洗澡时，患者把炉灶开着未关。

rows.en: Memory clinic follow-up is scheduled in 2 weeks.
rows.zh: 已预约2周后记忆门诊复诊。

rows.en: Two morning medication doses were found in a coffee cup.
rows.zh: 在咖啡杯中发现两次早晨药物未服。

rows.en: Daughter says, “I do not know how much longer I can do this.”
rows.zh: 女儿说：“我不知道自己还能这样撑多久。”

questions.topic: Caregiver burden after dementia discharge

stem.en: The daughter begins crying and says, “I promised I would never put Mom in a facility, but I am exhausted.” Which response by the nurse is most therapeutic?
stem.zh: 女儿开始哭泣并说：“我答应过永远不把妈妈送进机构，但我真的太累了。”护士哪项回应最具有治疗性？

correct.en: The best response validates the caregiver's distress and invites further assessment without judgment. It opens the door to discussing safety, respite, and realistic supports.
correct.zh: 最佳回应认可照护者的痛苦，并以不评判的方式进一步评估。这有助于讨论安全、喘息服务和现实可行的支持。

byChoice.en: This response shifts quickly to a task and does not first assess emotional distress or safety.
byChoice.zh: 该回应过快转向任务，没有先评估情绪痛苦或安全。

byChoice.en: This response supports therapeutic communication by naming the burden and inviting the caregiver to describe the hardest parts.
byChoice.zh: 该回应通过指出负担并邀请照护者描述最困难的部分，体现治疗性沟通。

byChoice.en: This response gives premature reassurance and may increase guilt by implying the caregiver should simply continue.
byChoice.zh: 该回应过早安慰，并可能暗示照护者应该继续坚持，从而加重内疚。

byChoice.en: This response is judgmental and frames facility care as failure rather than one possible safety resource.
byChoice.zh: 该回应带有评判性，把机构照护说成失败，而不是一种可能的安全资源。

testTakingStrategy.en: For psychosocial items, choose the response that validates feelings, avoids judgment, and keeps assessment open.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 心理社会题优先选择能认可感受、避免评判并继续评估的回应。

glossary.defZh: 帮助患者或家属表达感受、澄清问题并支持决策的沟通方式

options.en: “I will give you a list of agencies, and you can decide which one to call later.”
options.zh: “我给您一份机构清单，您之后自己决定打给哪家。”

options.en: “You sound overwhelmed. Tell me what has been hardest since your mother came home.”
options.zh: “听起来您已经非常疲惫。能告诉我母亲回家后最困难的是什么吗？”

options.en: “You are doing the right thing by keeping her at home, so try to take it one day at a time.”
options.zh: “您让她留在家里是正确的，所以尽量一天一天来。”

options.en: “A facility placement would break your promise, so we should avoid discussing that today.”
options.zh: “送机构会违背您的承诺，所以我们今天不谈这个。”

questions.topic: Caregiver burden after dementia discharge

stem.en: Which actions should the nurse include in the discharge follow-up plan? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 护士应将哪些措施纳入出院后随访计划？选择所有适用项。

correct.en: The plan should reduce immediate hazards, assess caregiver safety and coping, connect the family with respite and community supports, and simplify medication management. Telling the caregiver to wait or to promise constant supervision is unsafe and unrealistic.
correct.zh: 计划应减少即时危险，评估照护者安全和应对能力，连接喘息与社区资源，并简化用药管理。让照护者等待或承诺持续看护既不安全也不现实。

byChoice.en: Social work or case management can connect the family with respite, adult day programs, and eligibility resources.
byChoice.zh: 社工或个案管理可帮助家庭连接喘息服务、成人日间照护和资格资源。

byChoice.en: Waiting a month ignores current safety risks and caregiver strain.
byChoice.zh: 等待一个月会忽视当前安全风险和照护者压力。

byChoice.en: Removing or disabling stove access and adding door alerts address the wandering and kitchen-safety cues.
byChoice.zh: 移除或限制炉灶使用、增加门磁报警可处理走失和厨房安全线索。

byChoice.en: Expecting constant supervision without support is unrealistic and can worsen caregiver strain.
byChoice.zh: 要求无支持情况下持续看护不现实，并可能加重照护压力。

byChoice.en: Assessing the caregiver for depression, hopelessness, and safety concerns is appropriate because the exhibit shows exhaustion and distress.
byChoice.zh: 由于记录显示照护者疲惫和痛苦，评估抑郁、绝望和安全问题是适当的。

byChoice.en: Medication-management tools such as pill organizers, pharmacy packaging, or home-health teaching address missed doses.
byChoice.zh: 药盒、药房分装或居家护理教学等用药管理工具可处理漏服问题。

testTakingStrategy.en: Safe discharge planning matches each identified barrier with a realistic support, not a promise of perfect family supervision.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 安全出院计划应为每个障碍匹配现实可行的支持，而不是依赖家庭完美监督的承诺。

glossary.defZh: 临时替代照护，让主要照护者获得休息的服务
白天提供监督、活动和部分照护支持的社区项目

options.en: Request social work or case-management referral for respite and adult day services.
options.zh: 申请社工或个案管理转介，评估喘息服务和成人日间照护。

options.en: Advise the daughter to wait 1 month before changing the plan because adjustment takes time.
options.zh: 建议女儿先等1个月再改变计划，因为适应需要时间。

options.en: Discuss stove safety, door alerts, and a wandering-response plan.
options.zh: 讨论炉灶安全、门磁报警和走失应对计划。

options.en: Ask the daughter to promise that she will supervise the client continuously.
options.zh: 要求女儿承诺持续不断地看护患者。

options.en: Screen the caregiver for depression, hopelessness, and immediate safety concerns.
options.zh: 筛查照护者是否有抑郁、绝望和即时安全问题。

options.en: Arrange medication-management teaching and tools before the next refill.
options.zh: 在下次续药前安排用药管理教学和工具。

questions.topic: Caregiver burden after dementia discharge

stem.en: Place the nurse's follow-up actions in the safest order.
stem.zh: 将护士的随访措施按最安全的顺序排列。

correct.en: The nurse first assesses immediate danger, then addresses modifiable hazards and medication problems, then activates interdisciplinary support, and finally verifies the caregiver can explain the plan.
correct.zh: 护士应先评估即时危险，再处理可改变的环境危险和用药问题，然后启动跨专业支持，最后确认照护者能说明计划。

byChoice.en: Reducing stove, wandering, and medication hazards follows the safety assessment and targets concrete risks.
byChoice.zh: 在安全评估后，应减少炉灶、走失和用药危险，处理具体风险。

byChoice.en: Teach-back should verify that the caregiver understands whom to call and what to do, after the plan is developed.
byChoice.zh: 计划制定后，应通过回授确认照护者知道该联系谁、该做什么。

byChoice.en: Case-management and social-work referrals are needed once urgent safety needs are identified.
byChoice.zh: 识别紧急安全需求后，需要个案管理和社工转介。

byChoice.en: Immediate risk assessment comes first because the caregiver expressed severe exhaustion and the home has safety events.
byChoice.zh: 由于照护者表达严重疲惫且家中已有安全事件，首先应评估即时风险。

testTakingStrategy.en: In ordering questions, immediate safety and assessment come before education, referrals, and routine follow-up.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 排序题中，即时安全和评估先于教育、转介和常规随访。

glossary.defZh: 请学习者用自己的话复述计划，以确认理解程度

options.en: Identify specific home-safety and medication-management changes needed before the next night.
options.zh: 确定下一个夜晚前需要落实的居家安全和用药管理改变。

options.en: Use teach-back to confirm the daughter knows the warning signs and resource contacts.
options.zh: 使用回授法确认女儿知道警示信号和资源联系方式。

options.en: Contact case management/social work for respite, home-health, and community dementia resources.
options.zh: 联系个案管理/社工，安排喘息、居家护理和社区痴呆资源。

options.en: Assess for immediate danger, caregiver thoughts of self-harm, and whether the client can be safely supervised tonight.
options.zh: 评估即时危险、照护者是否有自伤想法，以及今晚患者能否被安全看护。

questions.topic: New ostomy discharge teaching with health literacy barriers

stem.en: Review the client record and answer the case-study items.
stem.zh: 查看患者病历，并回答病例题。

correct.en: This case emphasizes adapting ostomy discharge teaching to health literacy needs, confirming skill performance, protecting dignity, and ensuring supplies and escalation instructions are in place.
correct.zh: 本病例强调根据健康素养需求调整造口出院教学，确认操作技能，维护尊严，并确保用品和升级处理指示落实。

testTakingStrategy.en: When teaching fails, simplify the teaching method and ask the client to show understanding; do not label the client as noncompliant.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当教学无效时，应简化教学方法并请患者展示理解，不要把患者贴上“不依从”的标签。

glossary.defZh: 将结肠开口引至腹壁以排出粪便的手术造口
理解并使用健康信息进行自我护理的能力
造口周围与底盘和排出物接触的皮肤
专门指导造口护理和用品选择的护士

title.en: Case study: new ostomy teaching and health literacy
title.zh: 病例：新造口教学与健康素养

summary.en: A client with a new colostomy is preparing for discharge. The nurse notices signs that standard written instructions are not enough.
summary.zh: 一名新结肠造口患者即将出院。护士注意到标准书面说明不足以满足患者需要。

content.en:
Client has a new descending colostomy after surgery for bowel obstruction. Stoma is moist and beefy red. Peristomal skin is intact this morning.
Pouch output: soft brown stool. Client has been watching staff empty the pouch but has not done it independently.
content.zh:
患者因肠梗阻手术后建立新的降结肠造口。造口湿润、鲜红。今晨造口周围皮肤完整。
造口袋排出物：棕色软便。患者一直看工作人员排空造口袋，但尚未独立操作。

content.en:
The client nods during teaching but cannot explain when to empty the pouch or when to call the ostomy nurse.
Client quietly says, “I do not read too good. Please do not tell my daughter. She already thinks I cannot handle things.”
The printed discharge packet is 12 pages with small text. Starter supplies are at bedside; supply delivery has not been confirmed.
content.zh:
患者在教学时点头，但无法解释何时排空造口袋或何时联系造口护士。
患者小声说：“我不太会读。请不要告诉我女儿，她已经觉得我处理不了事情。”
打印的出院资料有12页，字很小。初始用品在床旁；用品配送尚未确认。

questions.topic: New ostomy discharge teaching with health literacy barriers

stem.en: Use the nurse note and discharge instructions to complete the statement.
stem.zh: 根据护士记录和出院指导完成句子。

correct.en: The exhibit shows the client is nodding but cannot describe the steps and is worried about reading. The nurse should use teach-back with plain language and demonstration rather than simply giving more written material.
correct.zh: 记录显示患者点头但不能说出步骤，并担心阅读能力。护士应使用通俗语言和示范的回授法，而不是只给更多书面材料。

byChoice.en: Health literacy barrier is correct because the client cannot use the written instructions and asks the nurse not to tell the family.
byChoice.zh: 健康素养障碍正确，因为患者无法使用书面说明，并要求护士不要告诉家人。

byChoice.en: Teach-back with demonstration is correct because it verifies understanding through the client showing and explaining the steps.
byChoice.zh: 示范式回授正确，因为它通过患者展示并解释步骤来确认理解。

testTakingStrategy.en: When a client says they understand but cannot explain or demonstrate care, treat it as a teaching-method problem, not a motivation problem.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当患者说懂了却不能解释或示范护理时，应把它视为教学方法问题，而不是动机问题。

glossary.defZh: 获得、理解并使用健康信息作出护理决定的能力
让患者用自己的话或动作展示所学内容以确认理解

clozeStem.en: The priority teaching problem is {{1}}, so the nurse should next use {{2}}.
clozeStem.zh: 优先的教学问题是 {{1}}，因此护士下一步应使用 {{2}}。

options.en: a health literacy barrier
options.zh: 健康素养障碍

options.en: intentional refusal of care
options.zh: 故意拒绝护理

options.en: normal independence after surgery
options.zh: 术后正常独立表现

options.en: teach-back with return demonstration
options.zh: 回授法并进行回示范

options.en: a longer written booklet
options.zh: 更长的书面手册

options.en: family teaching without the client present
options.zh: 让患者不在场时只教家属

questions.topic: New ostomy discharge teaching with health literacy barriers

stem.en: For each statement or finding, select the best interpretation.
stem.zh: 针对每句话或发现，选择最佳解释。

correct.en: A moist red stoma is expected early after surgery. A dusky stoma, repeated leakage with skin burning, and inability to explain pouch emptying need follow-up. Embarrassment requires supportive communication but is not the same as a physiologic complication.
correct.zh: 术后早期造口湿润呈红色是预期表现。造口发暗、反复漏袋伴皮肤灼痛，以及无法说明排空方法均需要跟进。尴尬需要支持性沟通，但不等同于生理并发症。

byChoice.en: A moist red stoma is an expected assessment finding.
byChoice.zh: 湿润红色造口是预期评估结果。

byChoice.en: A dusky or black stoma can indicate impaired blood flow and requires urgent follow-up.
byChoice.zh: 发暗或发黑的造口可提示血供受损，需要紧急跟进。

byChoice.en: Repeated leakage and burning skin can lead to skin breakdown and indicates the pouching system is not working.
byChoice.zh: 反复漏袋和皮肤灼痛可导致皮肤破损，提示造口袋系统不合适。

byChoice.en: The client's embarrassment is psychosocially important and should be addressed supportively, but it is not a stoma complication cue.
byChoice.zh: 患者尴尬在心理社会方面很重要，需要支持性处理，但不是造口并发症线索。

byChoice.en: Not knowing when to empty the pouch shows a discharge-readiness teaching gap.
byChoice.zh: 不知道何时排空造口袋提示出院准备教学存在缺口。

testTakingStrategy.en: Separate expected stoma findings from urgent circulation changes, skin-protection problems, and teaching gaps.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 将预期造口表现与紧急血运改变、皮肤保护问题和教学缺口区分开。

glossary.defZh: 手术在腹壁建立的肠道开口
造口周围与底盘或粪便接触的皮肤

rows.en: Stoma is moist and beefy red.
rows.zh: 造口湿润，呈鲜红色。

rows.en: Client says, “If the stoma turns dark purple or black, I will wait until the clinic visit.”
rows.zh: 患者说：“如果造口变成深紫色或黑色，我会等到门诊再说。”

rows.en: Pouch leaked twice overnight and the skin feels like it is burning.
rows.zh: 造口袋夜间漏了两次，皮肤感觉灼痛。

rows.en: Client says, “I feel embarrassed when I look at the pouch.”
rows.zh: 患者说：“我看到造口袋会觉得尴尬。”

rows.en: Client cannot state when to empty the pouch.
rows.zh: 患者说不出什么时候应排空造口袋。

questions.topic: New ostomy discharge teaching with health literacy barriers

stem.en: Which client statement indicates that discharge teaching about pouch emptying was effective?
stem.zh: 患者哪句话表明关于排空造口袋的出院教学有效？

correct.en: The correct statement reflects practical pouch management: emptying before the pouch becomes heavy helps prevent leakage and skin injury. The other statements delay care or show misunderstanding of normal care.
correct.zh: 正确陈述体现实际造口袋管理：在袋子过重前排空有助于预防漏袋和皮肤损伤。其他说法会延误处理或显示对正常护理理解错误。

byChoice.en: Waiting until the pouch is completely full increases pulling, leakage, odor, and skin irritation risk.
byChoice.zh: 等到完全满会增加牵拉、漏袋、异味和皮肤刺激风险。

byChoice.en: A moist red stoma is expected and should not be scrubbed; harsh cleaning can injure tissue.
byChoice.zh: 湿润红色造口是预期表现，不应刷洗；粗暴清洁会损伤组织。

byChoice.en: Emptying when the pouch is about one-third to one-half full reduces weight and leakage risk.
byChoice.zh: 造口袋约三分之一到二分之一满时排空，可减少重量和漏袋风险。

byChoice.en: Daily scheduled emptying alone is inadequate because output varies with intake and bowel pattern.
byChoice.zh: 仅每天固定排空不足，因为排出量会随进食和肠道模式变化。

testTakingStrategy.en: Evaluate teaching by looking for a statement that is specific, safe, and doable at home.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 评估教学效果时，寻找具体、安全且在家可执行的表述。

glossary.defZh: 造口排出物从底盘或袋体边缘漏出

options.en: “I will wait until the pouch is completely full so I do not waste supplies.”
options.zh: “我会等到造口袋完全满再排空，这样不浪费用品。”

options.en: “I should scrub the stoma if it looks red and moist.”
options.zh: “如果造口看起来红而湿，我应该用力刷洗。”

options.en: “I will empty the pouch when it is about one-third to one-half full.”
options.zh: “造口袋大约三分之一到二分之一满时，我就会排空。”

options.en: “I only need to empty the pouch once each morning.”
options.zh: “我只需要每天早上排空一次。”

questions.topic: New ostomy discharge teaching with health literacy barriers

stem.en: Which actions should the nurse take before discharge? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 出院前护士应采取哪些措施？选择所有适用项。

correct.en: Teaching should be adapted to the client's literacy needs, privacy preferences, and actual ability to perform care. The nurse should verify supply access and escalation instructions. Avoid excluding the client or relying on shame-based teaching.
correct.zh: 教学应适应患者健康素养需求、隐私偏好和实际操作能力。护士应确认用品获得途径和升级处理指示。应避免排除患者或采用羞辱式教学。

byChoice.en: Verifying supply delivery and contact numbers prevents unsafe gaps after discharge.
byChoice.zh: 确认用品配送和联系方式可防止出院后出现不安全断档。

byChoice.en: Family teaching should not occur behind the client's back unless the client lacks decision-making capacity and a legal representative is involved.
byChoice.zh: 除非患者缺乏决策能力且有法定代表，否则不应背着患者只教家属。

byChoice.en: Privacy should be respected while asking whom the client wants included in teaching.
byChoice.zh: 应尊重隐私，同时询问患者希望谁参与教学。

byChoice.en: Scaring the client with graphic images is not therapeutic and may worsen shame or avoidance.
byChoice.zh: 用图片吓唬患者不具治疗性，可能加重羞耻和回避。

byChoice.en: Plain-language, picture-based instructions match the client's reading concern.
byChoice.zh: 通俗语言和图片式说明符合患者的阅读顾虑。

byChoice.en: Return demonstration verifies psychomotor ability and understanding.
byChoice.zh: 回示范能确认操作能力和理解程度。

testTakingStrategy.en: Health-literacy interventions make information easier to use and verify understanding without blaming the learner.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 健康素养干预应让信息更容易使用，并在不责备学习者的情况下确认理解。

glossary.defZh: 让患者实际操作以确认技能掌握
避免专业术语、以清楚具体词语表达健康信息

options.en: Confirm the client has starter supplies, delivery arrangements, and a number to call for leakage or skin breakdown.
options.zh: 确认患者有初始用品、配送安排，以及漏袋或皮肤破损时可拨打的电话。

options.en: Teach the adult child outside the room because the client is embarrassed.
options.zh: 因为患者尴尬，就在病房外只教成年子女。

options.en: Ask permission to include a chosen support person in teaching.
options.zh: 征得同意后让患者选择的支持者参与教学。

options.en: Show severe skin-breakdown photos to make the client take pouching seriously.
options.zh: 展示严重皮肤破损照片，让患者重视造口袋护理。

options.en: Replace dense written instructions with plain-language steps and pictures.
options.zh: 用通俗语言步骤和图片替代密集书面说明。

options.en: Ask the client to demonstrate emptying and securing the pouch before leaving.
options.zh: 请患者离院前示范排空并固定造口袋。

questions.topic: chronic illness self-management and health literacy

stem.en: A nurse in an outpatient primary care clinic is preparing a self-management plan for a client with type 2 diabetes and low health literacy.
stem.zh: 门诊初级保健诊所的护士正在为一名患有2型糖尿病且健康素养较低的患者制定自我管理计划。

summary.en: The client has recently had medication changes, inconsistent meals, and increasing dependence on a spouse for daily health tasks.
summary.zh: 该患者近期调整了用药、进餐不规律，并且越来越依赖配偶完成日常健康管理任务。

title.zh: 制定宣教和协调护理计划

content.en: The provider asks the nurse to reinforce medication safety, assess barriers to meal consistency, and identify whether the spouse needs additional support. The clinic has a diabetes educator, dietitian, social worker, and bilingual printed teaching materials.
content.zh: 医嘱要求护士加强用药安全教育，评估规律进餐的障碍，并判断配偶是否需要更多支持。诊所有糖尿病教育护士、营养师、社工以及双语纸质宣教材料。

questions.topic: chronic illness self-management and health literacy

stem.en: For each finding, select whether it is expected, needs focused teaching, or needs prompt provider follow-up.
stem.zh: 对每项发现，选择其属于预期情况、需要重点宣教，还是需要及时联系医务人员跟进。

rows.en: The client takes glipizide when feeling shaky.
rows.zh: 患者发抖时服用格列吡嗪。

rows.en: The client skips breakfast on workdays.
rows.zh: 患者工作日不吃早餐。

rows.en: Fasting blood glucose readings include 64 and 69 mg/dL.
rows.zh: 空腹血糖记录包括64和69 mg/dL。

rows.en: Skin on both feet is intact.
rows.zh: 双足皮肤完整。

rows.en: The client prefers pictures with short instructions.
rows.zh: 患者偏好配图和简短说明。

columns.zh: 需要及时医务人员跟进

correct.en: Misusing glipizide and skipping meals require focused safety teaching. Recurrent fasting readings below 70 mg/dL require prompt provider follow-up because the medication plan may need adjustment. Intact foot skin and a stated learning preference are useful baseline findings.
correct.zh: 错误使用格列吡嗪和漏餐需要重点安全宣教。反复空腹血糖低于70 mg/dL需要及时联系医务人员，因为用药计划可能需要调整。足部皮肤完整和明确的学习偏好是有用的基线资料。

byChoice.en: Glipizide should not be used as a rescue medication for shakiness; shakiness may indicate hypoglycemia that needs fast-acting carbohydrate, not another glucose-lowering dose.
byChoice.zh: 格列吡嗪不应用作发抖时的“急救药”；发抖可能提示低血糖，需要快速作用的碳水化合物，而不是再服降糖药。

byChoice.en: Skipping meals while taking a sulfonylurea increases hypoglycemia risk and requires practical meal-planning teaching.
byChoice.zh: 服用磺脲类药物时漏餐会增加低血糖风险，需要进行切实可行的进餐计划宣教。

byChoice.en: Repeated fasting glucose values below 70 mg/dL are not simply a teaching issue; the prescriber should evaluate medication timing, dose, and meal pattern.
byChoice.zh: 反复空腹血糖低于70 mg/dL不只是宣教问题；开方者应评估用药时间、剂量和进餐模式。

byChoice.en: Intact skin is reassuring, although reduced sensation still requires preventive foot-care teaching.
byChoice.zh: 皮肤完整是令人放心的，但感觉减退仍需要预防性足部护理宣教。

byChoice.en: A stated preference for pictures and short instructions helps the nurse tailor teaching to the client's health literacy needs.
byChoice.zh: 患者说明偏好图片和简短说明，有助于护士根据其健康素养需要调整宣教方式。

testTakingStrategy.en: Separate cues that require teaching from cues that suggest the current treatment plan may be unsafe and needs provider review.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 区分需要宣教的线索和提示当前治疗计划可能不安全、需要医务人员复核的线索。

glossary.defZh: 一类刺激胰腺释放胰岛素的口服降糖药，可能导致低血糖。
理解并使用健康信息以作出护理决定的能力。
用于筛查足部保护性感觉是否减退的工具。

questions.topic: hypoglycemia prevention teaching

stem.en: Complete the statement about the priority teaching need.
stem.zh: 完成关于优先宣教需求的陈述。

clozeStem.en: The nurse should first teach the client to treat symptoms of {{1}} with {{2}}.
clozeStem.zh: 护士应首先教患者在出现 {{1}} 症状时使用 {{2}} 处理。

options.en: hypoglycemia
options.zh: 低血糖

options.en: hyperglycemia
options.zh: 高血糖

options.en: hypertension
options.zh: 高血压

options.en: fast-acting carbohydrate
options.zh: 快速作用的碳水化合物

options.en: an extra dose of glipizide
options.zh: 额外一剂格列吡嗪

options.en: a high-fat snack
options.zh: 高脂肪点心

correct.en: Shakiness with low fasting readings indicates hypoglycemia risk. The immediate teaching priority is to use fast-acting carbohydrate rather than taking more glucose-lowering medication.
correct.zh: 发抖且空腹血糖偏低提示低血糖风险。最优先的宣教是使用快速作用的碳水化合物，而不是再服降糖药。

byChoice.en: The client's shakiness, low readings, skipped meals, and sulfonylurea use point to hypoglycemia as the priority safety issue.
byChoice.zh: 患者发抖、血糖低、漏餐和使用磺脲类药物，均指向低血糖这一优先安全问题。

byChoice.en: Fast-acting carbohydrate is the appropriate first self-management action for suspected low blood glucose in an alert client who can swallow.
byChoice.zh: 对清醒且能吞咽的低血糖疑似患者，快速作用的碳水化合物是合适的首要自我处理措施。

testTakingStrategy.en: When a client describes symptoms after a medication change, connect the symptom pattern to the most immediate safety risk before choosing teaching content.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 当患者在用药调整后描述症状时，先把症状模式与最紧迫的安全风险联系起来，再选择宣教内容。

glossary.defZh: 血糖过低，可能出现发抖、出汗、心悸、饥饿或意识改变。

glossary.termZh: 快速作用的碳水化合物
glossary.defZh: 能迅速升高血糖的糖类来源，如葡萄糖片或含糖饮料。

questions.topic: teach-back for medication safety

stem.en: Place the nurse's teaching actions in the best order.
stem.zh: 将护士的宣教措施按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Clarify which self-management task the client most wants to handle without the spouse's help.
options.zh: 明确患者最希望不依赖配偶而自己完成的自我管理任务。

options.en: Use plain language and pictures to explain when to take glipizide and what to do if a meal is skipped.
options.zh: 用通俗语言和图片说明格列吡嗪何时服用，以及漏餐时应如何处理。

options.en: Ask the client to teach back the low-blood-glucose action plan using the labeled pillbox and picture sheet.
options.zh: 让患者借助贴好标签的药盒和图片单，复述低血糖处理计划。

options.en: Document the client's remaining barriers and arrange follow-up with the diabetes educator and dietitian.
options.zh: 记录患者仍存在的障碍，并安排糖尿病教育护士和营养师随访。

options.en: Ask the client to show how each medication is taken on a usual workday.
options.zh: 请患者演示平常工作日每种药物如何服用。

correct.en: The nurse first assesses the client's actual routine, then identifies a patient-centered goal, teaches using appropriate literacy supports, verifies understanding with teach-back, and finally documents barriers and coordinates follow-up.
correct.zh: 护士先评估患者真实的日常做法，再确定以患者为中心的目标，用适合其健康素养的方式宣教，使用回授法确认理解，最后记录障碍并协调随访。

byChoice.en: A client-centered goal increases engagement and addresses dependence on the spouse without removing needed support.
byChoice.zh: 以患者为中心的目标能提高参与度，并在不取消必要支持的情况下处理对配偶的依赖。

byChoice.en: Plain language and pictures match the client's learning preference and reduce medication-error risk.
byChoice.zh: 通俗语言和图片符合患者学习偏好，并能降低用药错误风险。

byChoice.en: Teach-back verifies whether the client can apply the plan with the actual tools that will be used at home.
byChoice.zh: 回授法能确认患者是否能借助家中实际使用的工具执行计划。

byChoice.en: After teaching is evaluated, remaining barriers and referrals are documented for continuity of care.
byChoice.zh: 评估宣教效果后，再记录仍存在的障碍和转介安排，以保证护理连续性。

byChoice.en: Assessment comes first; the nurse needs to know what the client actually does before teaching.
byChoice.zh: 评估应放在第一位；护士必须先了解患者实际如何做，才能进行宣教。

testTakingStrategy.en: For ordered teaching items, use the nursing process: assess, individualize, teach, evaluate, and coordinate follow-up.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 处理宣教排序题时，按护理程序思考：评估、个体化、宣教、评价、协调随访。

glossary.defZh: 让患者用自己的话或动作复述护理计划，以确认理解。
根据患者的优先事项、能力和生活情境共同制定的目标。

questions.topic: interprofessional diabetes care coordination

stem.en: Which actions should the nurse include in the care coordination plan? Select all that apply.
stem.zh: 护士应在护理协调计划中包括哪些措施？选择所有适用项。

options.en: Tell the spouse to continue managing all medications because the client has low health literacy.
options.zh: 告诉配偶继续管理所有药物，因为患者健康素养低。

options.en: Request a dietitian consult for a simple workday breakfast and snack plan.
options.zh: 申请营养师会诊，制定简单的工作日早餐和点心计划。

options.en: Offer social work support for caregiver strain and access to medication-labeling resources.
options.zh: 提供社工支持，以处理照护者压力并获取药物标签辅助资源。

options.en: Refer the client to the diabetes educator for medication and glucose-monitoring skills.
options.zh: 转介患者至糖尿病教育护士，学习用药和血糖监测技能。

options.en: Ask the provider whether recurrent low fasting readings require a medication-plan review.
options.zh: 询问医务人员反复空腹低血糖是否需要复核用药计划。

correct.en: The plan should build self-management skills, address recurrent low readings with the provider, support practical meal planning, and respond to caregiver strain. The nurse should not reinforce total dependence on the spouse or rely only on written instructions.
correct.zh: 计划应培养自我管理技能，联系医务人员处理反复低血糖，支持实际可行的进餐计划，并回应照护者压力。护士不应强化患者完全依赖配偶，也不应只依赖书面说明。

byChoice.en: Having the spouse manage everything may reduce immediate errors but increases dependence and does not support safe self-management.
byChoice.zh: 让配偶包办所有事情可能短期减少错误，但会增加依赖，不能支持安全的自我管理。

byChoice.en: Meal consistency is directly related to hypoglycemia prevention with sulfonylurea therapy.
byChoice.zh: 规律进餐与使用磺脲类药物时预防低血糖直接相关。

byChoice.en: The spouse expresses fear and burden; social work can help with caregiver support and practical resources.
byChoice.zh: 配偶表达害怕和负担；社工可协助照护者支持和实际资源。

byChoice.en: A diabetes educator can reinforce medication use, glucose monitoring, and problem solving using appropriate teaching methods.
byChoice.zh: 糖尿病教育护士可用合适的宣教方法强化用药、血糖监测和问题解决能力。

byChoice.en: Recurrent fasting readings below target while taking glipizide require provider review for possible medication changes.
byChoice.zh: 服用格列吡嗪期间反复空腹血糖低于目标范围，需要医务人员复核是否调整药物。

testTakingStrategy.en: Choose coordination actions that reduce risk while increasing the client's ability to participate safely in care.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 选择既能降低风险、又能提高患者安全参与护理能力的协调措施。

glossary.defZh: 照护任务造成的情绪、身体、经济或时间负担。
组织不同专业人员和资源，使患者获得连续、合适的护理。

questions.topic: self-management goal setting

stem.en: The client chooses a goal of walking 30 minutes per day on 5 days each week. How many minutes of walking per week should the nurse document for this goal? Enter a number only.
stem.zh: 患者选择的目标是每周5天、每天步行30分钟。护士应记录该目标为每周步行多少分钟？只输入数字。

correct.en: Thirty minutes per day multiplied by 5 days per week equals 150 minutes per week, a clear measurable self-management goal.
correct.zh: 每天30分钟乘以每周5天等于每周150分钟，这是明确可衡量的自我管理目标。

byChoice.en: Calculate 30 × 5 = 150 minutes per week.
byChoice.zh: 计算30 × 5 = 每周150分钟。

testTakingStrategy.en: For numeric goal-setting questions, identify the unit requested before multiplying or converting.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 处理数字目标设定题时，先确认题目要求的单位，再进行乘法或换算。

glossary.defZh: 可以用具体数字或行为判断是否完成的目标。
患者在日常生活中管理疾病、治疗和健康行为的能力。

content.en: A 58-year-old client arrives for follow-up 2 weeks after starting glipizide in addition to metformin. The client says, 'My wife reads the bottles for me. I take the small white pill when I feel shaky because I think it lowers the sugar faster.' The client reports skipping breakfast on workdays and keeping medications in a pillbox without labels. The spouse says, 'I am afraid to leave him alone because he does not know what to do if his sugar drops.'
content.zh: 58岁患者在二甲双胍基础上加用格列吡嗪2周后复诊。患者说：“药瓶都是我妻子帮我看的。我觉得发抖时就吃那颗小白药，因为我以为它能更快降糖。”患者报告工作日常不吃早餐，并将药物放在没有标签的药盒里。配偶说：“我不敢让他一个人在家，因为他不知道血糖低时该怎么办。”

content.en: Fasting blood glucose log for the last week: 64, 71, 132, 148, 69, 156, 138 mg/dL. Blood pressure 132/78 mm Hg. Foot exam: skin intact, reduced sensation to monofilament at both great toes. The client reads at about a fifth-grade level and prefers pictures with short instructions.
content.zh: 过去一周空腹血糖记录：64、71、132、148、69、156、138 mg/dL。血压132/78 mm Hg。足部检查：皮肤完整，双侧大脚趾单丝触觉减弱。患者阅读水平约为小学五年级，偏好配图和简短说明。

correct.en: This case emphasizes prevention, health literacy, medication safety, caregiver support, and coordinated chronic illness self-management.
correct.zh: 本病例强调预防、健康素养、用药安全、照护者支持以及慢性病自我管理的协调。

testTakingStrategy.en: In ambulatory chronic illness cases, prioritize safety risks, assess the client's actual routine, and use teach-back to verify learning.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在门诊慢性病病例中，优先处理安全风险，评估患者真实日常做法，并用回授法确认学习效果。

glossary.defZh: 患者长期管理症状、治疗、生活方式和随访的过程。
通过正确用药、监测和教育减少药物伤害的护理重点。

questions.topic: rehabilitation pressure injury prevention and bowel management

stem.en: A nurse on an inpatient rehabilitation unit is caring for an older adult after surgical repair of a hip fracture.
stem.zh: 住院康复病区护士正在护理一名髋部骨折术后老年患者。

summary.en: The client has limited mobility, intermittent urinary incontinence, constipation, sleep disruption, and family concerns about discharge readiness.
summary.zh: 患者活动受限，间歇性尿失禁，便秘，睡眠受干扰，家属担心出院准备不足。

content.en: Physical therapy will evaluate gait endurance this afternoon. Occupational therapy is available for bathroom equipment training. The provider prescribed scheduled stool softener and as-needed laxative. The unit has a toileting schedule protocol and pressure-injury prevention bundle.
content.zh: 物理治疗师将于今天下午评估步态耐力。作业治疗师可提供浴室设备训练。医嘱开立定时软便剂和必要时泻药。病区有定时如厕流程和压力性损伤预防组合措施。

questions.topic: pressure injury and comfort risk recognition

stem.en: Classify each finding based on the nurse's interpretation.
stem.zh: 根据护士的判断对每项发现进行分类。

rows.en: Sacrum is pink and blanches slowly.
rows.zh: 骶尾部发粉红色，按压后褪色缓慢。

rows.en: The client avoids fluids to prevent urinary leakage.
rows.zh: 患者为避免尿液渗漏而限制饮水。

rows.en: Last bowel movement was 4 days ago.
rows.zh: 上次排便为4天前。

rows.en: The client can stand with assistance and a walker.
rows.zh: 患者可在协助下使用助行器站立。

rows.en: The daughter works nights and fears the client will fall at home.
rows.zh: 女儿上夜班，并担心患者在家跌倒。

correct.en: Slow blanching over the sacrum, moisture, immobility, constipation, limited endurance, and family schedule barriers all affect the rehabilitation plan. Some findings fit more than one concern.
correct.zh: 骶尾部褪色缓慢、潮湿、活动受限、便秘、耐力不足以及家属时间安排障碍都会影响康复计划。有些发现同时属于多个问题。

byChoice.en: A slowly blanching pink sacrum signals tissue vulnerability and need for pressure-reduction measures.
byChoice.zh: 骶尾部发粉红且褪色缓慢提示组织易受损，需要减压措施。

byChoice.en: Fluid restriction can worsen constipation and hydration, while urinary leakage adds moisture that increases skin breakdown risk.
byChoice.zh: 限制饮水会加重便秘和水分不足，而尿液渗漏造成潮湿，会增加皮肤破损风险。

byChoice.en: No bowel movement for 4 days after surgery and reduced mobility is a bowel-management concern that needs intervention.
byChoice.zh: 术后活动减少且4天未排便，是需要干预的肠道管理问题。

byChoice.en: Limited endurance and needing assistance with a walker increase pressure risk from immobility and affect what support and equipment are needed after discharge.
byChoice.zh: 耐力有限且需要助行器协助，会因活动受限增加压力性损伤风险，并影响出院后所需支持和设备。

byChoice.en: The daughter's night work and fear of falls indicate a home-safety and caregiver-availability issue for discharge planning.
byChoice.zh: 女儿夜班工作且担心跌倒，提示出院计划中存在居家安全和照护者可用性问题。

testTakingStrategy.en: In rehabilitation cases, connect mobility, moisture, elimination, and caregiver availability rather than treating them as separate problems.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在康复病例中，要把活动能力、潮湿、排泄和照护者可用性联系起来，而不是割裂看待。

glossary.defZh: 按压皮肤后颜色暂时变浅，松开后颜色恢复的现象。
评估压力性损伤风险的工具，包含感觉、潮湿、活动等因素。

questions.topic: safe mobility and fall prevention

stem.en: The client needs to transfer from bed to a chair with a walker. Place the nursing actions in the best order.
stem.zh: 患者需要使用助行器从床转移到椅子。将护理措施按最佳顺序排列。

options.en: Ensure the call light and needed items are within reach after the client sits.
options.zh: 患者坐下后确认呼叫铃和所需物品在可及范围内。

options.en: Assist the client to sit at the bedside and assess dizziness before standing.
options.zh: 协助患者坐在床边，并在站立前评估是否头晕。

options.en: Confirm the chair is locked, close to the bed, and positioned on the client's stronger side when possible.
options.zh: 确认椅子已锁定、靠近床边，并尽可能放在患者较有力的一侧。

options.en: Apply nonskid footwear and a gait belt.
options.zh: 穿防滑鞋并系好步态带。

options.en: Cue the client to push from the bed, stand, grasp the walker, and pivot toward the chair.
options.zh: 提示患者从床面推起站立，握住助行器，并转身朝向椅子。

correct.en: The nurse first prepares the environment, then prepares the client, assesses tolerance in sitting, assists the stand-pivot transfer safely, and finally restores access to the call light and needed items.
correct.zh: 护士先准备环境，再准备患者，坐起后评估耐受性，安全协助站立转移，最后确保呼叫铃和所需物品可及。

byChoice.en: After the transfer, keeping the call light and items within reach prevents unassisted attempts to stand.
byChoice.zh: 转移后使呼叫铃和物品在可及范围内，可预防患者无人协助时自行站起。

byChoice.en: Sitting and checking for dizziness before standing reduces orthostatic and fall risk.
byChoice.zh: 站立前先坐起并评估头晕，可降低体位性不适和跌倒风险。

byChoice.en: Environmental setup comes first because an unlocked or poorly positioned chair creates a fall risk during the transfer.
byChoice.zh: 环境准备应最先完成，因为椅子未锁定或位置不当会在转移时造成跌倒风险。

byChoice.en: Nonskid footwear and a gait belt prepare the client for safer assisted mobility.
byChoice.zh: 防滑鞋和步态带能让患者在协助下更安全地活动。

byChoice.en: The client should push from the bed rather than pull on the walker, then pivot with assistance toward the chair.
byChoice.zh: 患者应从床面推起，而不是拉助行器起身，然后在协助下转向椅子。

testTakingStrategy.en: For mobility sequences, prepare the environment before moving the client and reassess safety after the move.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 处理活动转移排序题时，先准备环境再移动患者，移动后重新评估安全。

glossary.defZh: 协助患者站立或行走时使用的安全辅助带。
患者站起后转身坐到另一支撑面的转移动作。

questions.topic: pressure injury prevention bundle

stem.en: Complete the prevention plan statement.
stem.zh: 完成预防计划陈述。

clozeStem.en: The client is at increased risk for {{1}} because of {{2}}; the nurse should prioritize {{3}}.
clozeStem.zh: 由于 {{2}}，患者发生 {{1}} 的风险增加；护士应优先采取 {{3}}。

options.en: pressure injury
options.zh: 压力性损伤

options.en: fluid overload
options.zh: 液体过多

options.en: airborne infection
options.zh: 空气传播感染

options.en: limited mobility and moisture from urinary leakage
options.zh: 活动受限和尿液渗漏造成潮湿

options.en: night-shift work by the daughter
options.zh: 女儿上夜班

options.en: the prescribed stool softener
options.zh: 已开立软便剂

options.en: scheduled repositioning, moisture management, and pressure redistribution
options.zh: 定时变换体位、管理潮湿并分散压力

options.en: limiting oral fluids until urinary leakage stops
options.zh: 在尿液渗漏停止前限制口服液体

options.en: keeping the head of bed elevated at all times
options.zh: 始终保持床头抬高

correct.en: Limited mobility, moisture, and early sacral color changes place the client at risk for pressure injury. Prevention should combine repositioning, moisture control, and pressure redistribution rather than fluid restriction or constant head elevation.
correct.zh: 活动受限、潮湿以及骶尾部早期颜色改变使患者有压力性损伤风险。预防应结合变换体位、控制潮湿和分散压力，而不是限制饮水或始终抬高床头。

byChoice.en: The key skin concern is pressure injury because the client has immobility, moisture, and sacral tissue changes.
byChoice.zh: 主要皮肤问题是压力性损伤，因为患者存在活动受限、潮湿和骶尾部组织改变。

byChoice.en: Limited mobility and moisture are direct, modifiable contributors to pressure injury risk.
byChoice.zh: 活动受限和潮湿是压力性损伤风险的直接、可干预因素。

byChoice.en: A bundled approach addresses pressure, shear, and moisture more effectively than a single intervention.
byChoice.zh: 组合措施比单一措施更能同时处理压力、剪切力和潮湿。

testTakingStrategy.en: For prevention questions, choose interventions that address the cause of risk rather than a symptom in isolation.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 处理预防题时，选择针对风险原因的措施，而不是孤立处理某个症状。

glossary.defZh: 通过床垫、坐垫或体位调整减少局部组织受压。
保持皮肤清洁干燥并减少尿液、粪便或汗液对皮肤的刺激。

questions.topic: intake calculation for bowel and bladder planning

stem.en: During the day shift, the client drinks 600 mL of water, 8 oz of milk, and 0.5 L of soup. Using 1 oz = 30 mL, how many total mL of intake should the nurse document? Enter a number only.
stem.zh: 白班期间，患者饮水600 mL、牛奶8 oz、汤0.5 L。按1 oz = 30 mL计算，护士应记录总摄入量多少mL？只输入数字。

correct.en: Eight ounces equals 240 mL, and 0.5 L equals 500 mL. Total intake is 600 + 240 + 500 = 1340 mL.

byChoice.en: Convert all amounts to milliliters before adding: 600 mL + 240 mL + 500 mL = 1340 mL.
byChoice.zh: 先全部换算为毫升再相加：600 mL + 240 mL + 500 mL = 1340 mL。

testTakingStrategy.en: Convert each intake amount to the same unit before adding; do not mix ounces, liters, and milliliters.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 先把每一项摄入量换算为同一单位再相加；不要混用盎司、升和毫升。

glossary.defZh: 患者经口或其他途径进入体内的液体量。
记录液体摄入和排出常用的容量单位。

questions.topic: delegation and interprofessional rehabilitation coordination

stem.en: For each care activity, select the most appropriate team member or role.
stem.zh: 对每项护理活动，选择最合适的团队成员或角色。

rows.en: Assist the client to the toilet using the established transfer plan.
rows.zh: 按已建立的转移计划协助患者如厕。

rows.en: Evaluate whether the client's sacral skin changes are worsening.
rows.zh: 评估患者骶尾部皮肤改变是否加重。

rows.en: Teach the daughter how to set up a raised toilet seat and remove trip hazards.
rows.zh: 教女儿如何安装加高马桶座并清除绊倒隐患。

rows.en: Assess gait endurance and update the walker-distance goal.
rows.zh: 评估步态耐力并更新助行距离目标。

rows.en: Review bowel pattern, fluid intake, and response to the prescribed bowel regimen.
rows.zh: 复核排便模式、液体摄入量以及对医嘱排便方案的反应。

correct.en: Assistive personnel may perform established, routine mobility assistance. The RN assesses skin and bowel response. Physical therapy evaluates gait endurance, while occupational therapy focuses on equipment and safe performance of activities of daily living.
correct.zh: 辅助护理人员可执行已建立的常规活动协助。注册护士评估皮肤和肠道反应。物理治疗评估步态耐力，作业治疗侧重设备使用和日常生活活动安全。

byChoice.en: Once the transfer plan is established and the client is stable, assistive personnel may help with toileting and report changes.
byChoice.zh: 转移计划已建立且患者稳定后，辅助护理人员可协助如厕并报告变化。

byChoice.en: Evaluating whether skin changes are worsening requires RN assessment and clinical judgment.
byChoice.zh: 判断皮肤改变是否加重需要注册护士评估和临床判断。

byChoice.en: Occupational therapy addresses bathroom equipment, environmental modification, and activities of daily living.
byChoice.zh: 作业治疗负责浴室设备、环境改造和日常生活活动训练。

byChoice.en: Physical therapy evaluates mobility endurance and progression of gait goals.
byChoice.zh: 物理治疗负责评估活动耐力并推进步态目标。

byChoice.en: The RN evaluates bowel regimen effectiveness and hydration patterns and decides whether further provider follow-up is needed.
byChoice.zh: 注册护士评估排便方案效果和水分摄入模式，并判断是否需要进一步联系医务人员。

testTakingStrategy.en: Delegate routine, predictable tasks; keep assessment, evaluation, and care-plan revision with licensed professionals.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 可委派常规且可预测的任务；评估、评价和护理计划修订应由有执照专业人员负责。

glossary.defZh: 在护士监督下执行已委派的基本护理任务的人员。
帮助患者恢复或适应日常生活活动和安全使用设备的康复专业。

content.en: A 76-year-old client is postoperative day 5 after open reduction and internal fixation of a left hip fracture. The client can stand with assistance and a walker but tires quickly. The client says, 'I avoid drinking because I do not want to wet the bed.' The adult daughter says, 'I work nights and I am scared Mom will fall if she tries to go to the bathroom alone.'
content.zh: 76岁患者左髋骨折切开复位内固定术后第5天。患者可在协助下使用助行器站立，但很快疲劳。患者说：“我不敢喝水，因为不想尿床。”成年女儿说：“我上夜班，我担心妈妈如果自己去厕所会跌倒。”

content.en: Braden risk factors: limited mobility, occasional moisture from urinary leakage, fair nutrition, and needs assistance with repositioning. Sacrum is pink and blanches slowly. Last bowel movement was 4 days ago. Client reports sleeping 3 to 4 hours because staff wake the client for nonurgent tasks.
content.zh: Braden风险因素：活动受限、偶有尿液渗漏导致潮湿、营养尚可、翻身需要协助。骶尾部发粉红色，按压后褪色缓慢。上次排便为4天前。患者报告因工作人员进行非紧急操作而被叫醒，夜间只睡3到4小时。

correct.en: This case emphasizes rehabilitation nursing, pressure injury prevention, bowel and bladder management, sleep promotion, fall prevention, delegation, and discharge planning.
correct.zh: 本病例强调康复护理、压力性损伤预防、肠膀胱管理、促进睡眠、预防跌倒、委派和出院计划。

testTakingStrategy.en: In rehabilitation cases, prioritize prevention, functional safety, comfort, and coordinated discharge resources.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在康复病例中，优先考虑预防、功能安全、舒适以及协调出院资源。

glossary.defZh: 帮助患者恢复功能、预防并发症并安全回归日常生活的护理。
患者和照护者是否具备安全离院所需的知识、能力、设备和支持。

questions.topic: caregiver burden and family adaptation

stem.en: A home-health nurse is visiting a client with dementia and assessing the spouse's ability to continue caregiving safely.
stem.zh: 家庭护理护士正在探访一名患有痴呆症的患者，并评估其配偶是否能继续安全地提供照护。

summary.en: The client has progressive cognitive impairment, nighttime wandering, missed medications, and a spouse who reports exhaustion and fear of losing control.
summary.zh: 患者认知功能逐渐下降，夜间徘徊，漏服药物；配偶报告精疲力竭，并担心自己会失控。

content.en: A 74-year-old client with moderate dementia lives with the spouse in a two-story home. The spouse says, "He wakes up at night looking for his mother and tries to leave the house. Yesterday I yelled and held his wrists to stop him. I am scared I will lose control." The spouse reports sleeping 3 to 4 hours per night and has not attended a caregiver support group.
content.zh: 74岁中度痴呆症患者与配偶住在一栋两层住宅内。配偶说：“他夜里醒来找妈妈，还想离家。昨天我大喊并抓住他的手腕阻止他。我害怕自己会失控。”配偶报告每晚只睡3到4小时，且没有参加照护者支持小组。

content.en: The front door has no alarm or bell. The smoke alarm in the hallway was disabled after repeated false alarms during cooking. Throw rugs are present between the bedroom and bathroom. The weekly medication organizer has two missed morning doses of the client's antihypertensive medication. No bruising or injury is observed on the client's wrists.
content.zh: 前门没有报警器或铃铛。走廊烟雾报警器因做饭时反复误响而被关闭。卧室和浴室之间有小地毯。每周药盒中有两次早晨降压药漏服。患者手腕未见瘀伤或损伤。
